display_name: Standing Rock Sioux Tribe — North Dakota / South Dakota

sources.population: U.S. Census Bureau. American Community Survey 2020-2024. Sioux County ND + Corson County SD combined service area population ~8,250.
sources.ethnicity: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 2020-2024. Sioux County ND: American Indian/Alaska Native 84.7% of population. Remaining: white 12.1%, other 3.2%.
sources.sex_ratio: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 2020-2024. Sioux County ND female proportion: 50.2%.
sources.age_distribution: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 2020-2024. Sioux County ND median age 28.4 years. ICU stroke cohort age bands recalibrated per Hangad 2026 clinical review (HSX-STROKE-CALIB-001): 60% 65-90, 40% 45-64. Targets mean age 65-68, under-50 capped at ~13%. General community median age 28.4 not applicable to stroke ICU population.
sources.diabetes: Indian Health Service. IHS Trends in Indian Health 2014-2015. Aberdeen Area (ND/SD) diabetes prevalence: 14.5% age-adjusted vs 6.4% US general population. CDC IHS data 2022 update: AI/AN diabetes prevalence 14.7% vs 7.5% non-Hispanic white.
sources.hypertension: Espey DK et al. Leading causes of death and all-cause mortality in American Indians and Alaska Natives. Am J Public Health. 2014;104(S3):S303-S311. Cardiovascular disease mortality 1.2x higher than US all-races. HTN prevalence estimated ~35-40% adult AI/AN.
sources.sepsis_burden: Brinkworth JF, Shaw JG. On race, human variation, and who gets and dies of sepsis. Am J Biol Anthropol. 2022;178(S74):230-255. Indigenous people disproportionately overrepresented in sepsis incidence and mortality in US.
sources.rural_flag: Standing Rock IHS Service Unit. Rural designation: HRSA Rural Health Grants Eligibility. All ZIP codes in service area rural.
sources.IHS_context: Indian Health Service Aberdeen Area. Standing Rock Service Unit, Fort Yates ND. Federal trust responsibility healthcare provider. Air-gapped network infrastructure common.
sources.sepsis_vitals: Singer M et al. Sepsis-3. JAMA. 2016;315(8):801-810.
sources.afebrile_elderly: Gavazzi G, Krause KH. Ageing and infection. Lancet Infect Dis. 2002;2(11):659-666.
sources.infection_source: Rhee C et al. JAMA Intern Med. 2017;177(7):944-951. Rural adjustment applied.
sources.lab_floors: WBC: Singer 2016 Sepsis-3. Glucose: ADA 2024. Creatinine: KDIGO 2022. LDL: Grundy 2019 ACC/AHA.

ihs_service_unit: Standing Rock Service Unit, Fort Yates ND
calibration_notes: Diabetes prevalence 14.7% in AI/AN vs 7.5% non-Hispanic white — engine does not yet apply regional comorbidity override; documented gap
Sepsis infection source weighted toward pneumonia for rural designation per Rhee 2017

Age bands recalibrated for ICU stroke cohort per Hangad 2026 (HSX-STROKE-CALIB-001): [[45,64,0.40],[65,90,0.60]]. Targets mean age 65-68, under-50 ~13%. Community median 28.4 is a general population stat not representative of ICU stroke patients.
HTN burden elevated in AI/AN relative to engine US baseline — not regionally adjusted; documented gap
Air-gapped IHS network is primary deployment target for this dataset

continuous_vars._source: IHS Trends in Indian Health 2014-2015; NHANES AI/AN oversampling; Strong Heart Study ND/SD data.

hba1c.source: IHS Aberdeen Area; AI/AN DM prevalence 14.7% (CDC 2022); highest HbA1c burden vs US mean

bmi.source: BRFSS AI/AN oversampling; Strong Heart Study. High obesity burden in Standing Rock population.

systolic_bp.source: Strong Heart Study ND/SD AI/AN; Espey 2014; estimated 35-40% HTN AI/AN

creatinine.source: NHANES AI/AN CKD data; higher CKD burden from DM+HTN comorbidity; KDIGO 2022

egfr.source: IHS CKD surveillance; CKD-EPI 2021; elevated CKD stage 3+ in AI/AN DM+HTN population

temporal_trends._source: IHS trend data; CDC AI/AN surveillance; CMS Part D AI/AN utilization; BRFSS AI/AN oversampling

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 6-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 6-month drift interval

6.source: IHS trends 2024; CDC AI/AN Diabetes Surveillance; BRFSS AI/AN 2022

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 12-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 12-month drift interval

12.source: IHS trends 2024; CDC AI/AN Diabetes Surveillance; BRFSS AI/AN 2022

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 18-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 18-month drift interval

18.source: IHS trends 2024; CDC AI/AN Diabetes Surveillance; BRFSS AI/AN 2022
